understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to complete each level by moving the selectable piece(s) so that every goal tile marked with a '+' in the semantic grid is occupied by a selectable simultaneously, within the per-level step budget; achieving WIN on the final level completes the game.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement actions (UP/DOWN/LEFT/RIGHT) attempt to shift the currently selected piece by one grid cell (3 pixels) in that direction; moves can fail if blocked by walls/boundaries or other objects, though the movement_blocked hints are only heuristic and don’t guarantee failure. Pushable blocks can be moved if adjacent in the movement direction and space allows, moving together with the selected piece. Levels can contain multiple selectable pieces; CLICK selects a different piece by providing its exact pixel-grid position as listed in the state (same coordinate system as player.position and objects). Walls generally prevent movement/pushing, but their exact behavior may vary and must be inferred from interactions.
